title: +7.25R (14k @ 2% Risk) in May on Claude Opus. 26 trades, 6 markets.
author: The_AI_Trader
selftext:
So this is the part of the build i did not expect to get this into. i run a separate AI trader on each market, six of them, eurusd gbpusd usdjpy nas100 us500 and us30, all going independently. may closed at +7.25R across 26 trades for the group. 16 wins, 10 losses, 61.5%. on a 100k account at 2% a trade thats about 14.5k for the month. but the number isnt even the interesting part to me. its that they act like a little team where every one has its own personality and some of them just dont show up some months.

the fx ones carried it this month. eurusd was on a tear, every winner a short and all the exact same move, a bounce that dies at the session VWAP with a fib sitting right at the same price, fire on the rejection. gbpusd ran basically the identical playbook and usdjpy did the long version into pre defined pullback zones. those three basically paid for everyone elses mistakes.

and then theres us30. same setup, same discipline, completly different outcome lol. one winner the whole month and then it just kept buying pullbacks that didnt hold and eating -1R stops, may 5, 18, 27, boom boom boom. if you only watched us30 you'd swear the whole system was broken. which is kinda the whole point of running them seperate, one market dies and you dont even feel it cause the others are working.

The thing that surprised me is they all have their own setup but theyre all variations of one idea. none of them chase. ever. every single one waits for a pullback to a level and fires on the rejection, never an entry on the fresh high or low. they just dont agree on what a level even is. eurusd treats VWAP like its law. nas ignores it completely and uses fib and the EMA. same religion different churches basically.

Best example of the no chasing thing is a usdjpy long on the 26th. chasing the breakout at 159.275 wouldve been 0.31R. waiting for the pullback at 159.248 was 1.14R. same trade, same target, like 4x the return for waiting 14 min.

anyway running a spread of agents like this is way more fun to watch than the single bot i had before. the diversification is real and the personalities are weirdly real too. anyone else running separate agents per instrument instead of one model across everything? curious how yours holds up when one of your markets turns on you.

Having said that, what a kick ass month!
